patterns.fingerprint: const orig = w.callback; w.callback = function(v) {...; orig?.call(this,v)}
patterns.semantic: subscribe to widget value change
patterns.v2_replacement: widget.on('change', fn)

evidence.notes: 14 instances in one file
uses .bind(directoryWidget) — callbacks lose `this` when reassigned
replaces without preserving original — silently breaks other extensions
tempCallback swap inside the callback — race / re-entrancy hazard

derived.verdict: Replace before removing — 4 of our own extensions need migration

patterns.semantic: intercept node serialization to inject custom workflow JSON fields
patterns.v2_replacement: node.on('beforeSerialize', (workflow) => {...}) OR widget.setSerializeValue (per dynamicPrompts.v2)
patterns.decision_ref: D3.3 + open question in extensionV2Service comments

evidence.notes: internal docs teach this anti-pattern as the fix

patterns.semantic: react when a node is hydrated from a saved workflow (effective replacement for unused loadedGraphNode hook)
patterns.v2_replacement: defineNodeExtension({ loadedGraphNode(node) {...} }) — with LoadedFromWorkflow tag (D3.5)

evidence.notes: OUR OWN ECOSYSTEM — uses LGraphNode.prototype directly, not nodeType
OUR OWN CORE uses useChainCallback helper — DEP2
OUR OWN CORE — patches SubgraphNode.prototype, not just LGraphNode

patterns.semantic: react when a node is added to a graph (vs onNodeCreated which fires before graph attachment)
patterns.v2_replacement: defineNodeExtension({ nodeCreated }) — semantic difference: needs an additional 'attached to graph' event OR consolidate

evidence.notes: async function — onAdded callback is awaited

patterns.surface: nodeType.prototype.onDrawForeground patching (custom canvas drawing per node)
patterns.semantic: draw custom 2D canvas content on the node (badges, indicators, image previews, sliders)
patterns.v2_replacement: NEW — needs canvas drawing API. Could be defineNodeExtension({ onDraw(node, ctx) }) but lifecycle coupling is HIGH

evidence.notes: ECOSYSTEM TEACHES THIS — Cursor rules file documents 'Hijack nodeType.prototype.onDrawForeground in beforeRegisterNodeDef' as canonical
uses node_type instead of nodeType — typo or local convention

patterns.semantic: intercept mouse events on a node (custom click handlers, drag-to-resize regions)
patterns.v2_replacement: node.on('mouseDown', (event, pos) => {...}) OR widget-level pointer events

patterns.semantic: force a custom node size (typically for DOM-widget-heavy nodes)
patterns.v2_replacement: defineNodeExtension({ minSize: [w, h] }) declarative — must call LiteGraph.LGraphNode.prototype.computeSize.call(this, out) for super behavior

evidence.notes: uses LiteGraph.LGraphNode.prototype.computeSize.call(this, out) — couples to global namespace

patterns.fingerprint: const orig = app.graphToPrompt; app.graphToPrompt = async function(...args) {...; return orig(...args)}
patterns.semantic: intercept workflow→API serialization to inject virtual node resolution, custom metadata, or share data
patterns.v2_replacement: graph.on('beforeSerialize', workflow => mutate(workflow))  OR  app.on('beforePrompt', payload => mutate(payload))

evidence.notes: KJNodes — one of the most-installed packs. Patching graphToPrompt to resolve virtual set/get nodes into real connections
OUR OWN ComfyUI-Manager — most-installed extension in the entire ecosystem. Patches graphToPrompt for component manager.

patterns.semantic: external/embed scenario — load a workflow JSON into the running editor from outside extension scope
patterns.v2_replacement: app.loadWorkflow(json)  exposed on the public API barrel

evidence.notes: from another window context — sibling-tab orchestration
BennyKok/comfyui-deploy is widely used in cloud-deployment scenarios
OUR OWN TESTS depend on window.app.graphToPrompt — confirms this surface must remain

patterns.semantic: subscribe to backend execution progress (per-node steps, percentage)
patterns.v2_replacement: node.on('progress', ({ step, total, value }) => ...)  AND  app.on('progress', ...) for global

evidence.notes: PROMPT_SERVICE.addEventListener — different API path
OUR test framework subscribes to progress_text event

patterns.semantic: transform widget value at workflow serialization time (dynamic prompts, hidden state, expand-on-save)
patterns.v2_replacement: widget.setSerializeValue(fn)  (already in extensionV2.ts WidgetHandle)

evidence.notes: OUR OWN TEST verifies serializeValue is a function — confirms shape contract
third-party skill teaches the (node, index) signature

patterns.surface: ComfyExtension.getCanvasMenuItems hook (the v1 alternative to canvas-prototype patching)
patterns.semantic: contribute items to the canvas right-click menu — the SUPPORTED replacement for LGraphCanvas.prototype.getCanvasMenuOptions patching
patterns.v2_replacement: defineCanvasExtension({ menuItems: [...] }) (NEW G13)

evidence.notes: uses the hook AND prototype-patches LGraphCanvas in setup — extension flips a 'newAPIAvailable' flag based on which fires first. Reveals: extensions distrust the hook, fall back to prototype patching.
4 core extensions use this hook — confirm by grep

derived.verdict: Keep but redesign — current hook returns mutable array, like P6 menu options. Needs declarative shape with idempotence.

patterns.semantic: contribute items to a node's right-click menu — supported replacement for getExtraMenuOptions prototype patching
patterns.v2_replacement: defineNodeExtension({ menuItems: [...] }) declarative

evidence.notes: calls window._eclipseBuildNodeMenuItems — extension uses window globals for cross-file communication
4 core extensions use this hook — confirm by grep

patterns.semantic: register pure-frontend node types (no backend Python class)
patterns.v2_replacement: BLOCKER — D4-G2 — needs explicit registerCustomNodes-equivalent

evidence.notes: the SAME single user as registerCustomNodes and loadedGraphNode — sofakid/dandy uses ALL THREE rare hooks, suggests they're a sophisticated extension that needs the full surface
1 of 23 core extensions uses this — find which

patterns.semantic: register entirely virtual nodes (rerouteNode, noteNode in our core)
patterns.v2_replacement: BLOCKER — D4-G2 — virtual-node registration API

evidence.notes: OUR OWN CORE — rerouteNode uses this, unmigratable in current v2
OUR OWN CORE — noteNode uses this, unmigratable in current v2

patterns.semantic: intercept incoming connection BEFORE it is wired (return false to refuse, mutate slots, type-coerce)
patterns.v2_replacement: node.on('beforeConnect', { slot, peer } => false|true)

evidence.notes: OUR OWN CORE — dynamicWidgets uses instance assignment, not prototype
KJNodes (★2,567) — first canonical occurrence with full 5-arg signature
second onConnectInput patch in same file — multiple node types in one pack

patterns.semantic: intercept outgoing connection BEFORE it is wired (mirror of onConnectInput)
patterns.v2_replacement: node.on('beforeConnect', { slot, peer } => false|true)

evidence.notes: rgthree — major pack. Subclass override pattern (calls super).

patterns.semantic: react to widget value changes at the node level (vs widget.callback at widget level)
patterns.v2_replacement: node.on('widgetChanged', { name, newValue, oldValue, widget } => ...)

evidence.notes: OUR OWN CORE — widgetInputs invokes onWidgetChanged?.()
OUR OWN TESTS — extensive test coverage; this hook is contractually relied on internally
captures original onWidgetChanged via prototype chain

evidence.excerpt:
const input = theirNode.inputs[link.target_slot]
        if (!input || !input.widget || theirNode.widgets === undefined) return

        node.outputs[0].type = input.type

        if (node.widgets[0].value === "Parameter") {
            node.widgets[0].value = input.name
        }

        const widgetName = input.widget.name
        const theirWidget = theirNode.widgets.find((w) => w.name === widgetName)
        if (!theirWidget) return // connected to a custom node that isn't installed

        const widgetType = theirWidget.origType ?? theirWidget.type
        changeWidgets(node, widgetType, theirNode, theirWidget)

patterns.semantic: extensions subscribe to backend execution lifecycle WebSocket events
patterns.v2_replacement: ctx.execution.on('start' | 'success' | 'error' | 'cached', payload => ...) typed events

evidence.notes: C# wrapper enumerates the WebSocket event vocabulary as the public API
explicit 'Sidecar-like tracing' depending on execution_* events as public API

patterns.surface: graph.beforeChange / graph.afterChange — explicit batching seam for multi-step mutations
patterns.semantic: extensions wrap multi-node/multi-link mutations in beforeChange/afterChange so undo, dirty-tracking, and re-render coalesce around the batch instead of per-mutation
patterns.v2_replacement: world.batch(() => { ...mutations... }) — typed batching API
patterns.decision_ref: First-class batching is required for any reactive layer that wants stable diffs; v2 should expose this as a mandatory wrapper for multi-mutation operations

evidence.notes: docs use beforeChange/afterChange around subgraph promotion
paste flow batches mutations across clipboard restore
external doc treats beforeChange/afterChange as part of subgraph contract
batches paste-multiple-nodes into single beforeChange/afterChange envelope
bundled core override — confirms pattern travels through vendored builds

patterns.surface: window.LiteGraph / window.comfyAPI.* — globals as public surface
patterns.semantic: extensions reach into the global namespace for LiteGraph constructors/enums or for the module-as-global comfyAPI registry. This is the closest thing to a 'public ABI' today
patterns.v2_replacement: explicit `import { app, graph, LiteGraph } from '@comfy/extension'` + a typed registry keyed by extension name; window.* should remain as a deprecated read-only mirror
patterns.decision_ref: Cannot break window.LiteGraph immediately — too much ecosystem code reaches for it. Must ship typed import path first, then deprecate. Similar story to S11.G2 graph globals.

patterns.semantic: after any imperative mutation extensions call setDirtyCanvas to force a redraw — the ecosystem's de-facto 'reactivity flush' primitive. v2 reactivity should make this unnecessary
patterns.v2_replacement: implicit — reactive system schedules redraw automatically when tracked entity mutates. Provide an escape hatch `world.markDirty()` only for non-reactive third-party canvas use
patterns.decision_ref: Replacing this surface is the strongest evidence that v2 reactivity actually buys something. Should be in v2 'value proposition' demo extension

evidence.notes: multiple call sites — extension assumes manual flush is the contract

patterns.surface: node.setSize(node.computeSize()) — imperative resize after dynamic mutation
patterns.semantic: after dynamic widget/input/output mutation, extensions manually call computeSize+setSize to reflow the node. Companion to S2.N11 (computeSize override) and S11.G4 (setDirtyCanvas)
patterns.v2_replacement: automatic — reactive layout system recomputes node size when widget/slot collection changes. Expose `nodeHandle.requestLayout()` only as escape hatch
patterns.decision_ref: Pairs with S11.G4 — both are 'manual flush' idioms that v2 should obviate

evidence.notes: exact 'node.setSize?.(node.computeSize())' canonical idiom
commented-out manual setSize — shows the pattern is well-known
exact 'node.setSize(node.computeSize())' — third confirmed pack
preserves width, recomputes height — common variant of the idiom
passes current size to computeSize for width-aware height calc
ffmpeg preview pack — multiple call sites in one file
deferred setSize via setTimeout — workaround for layout race
Easy-Use pack exports updateNodeHeight(node) helper — confirms the idiom is a documented utility
post-clone relayout; Easy-Use is a major ★2,500+ pack

patterns.surface: SVG with onload/onerror attribute (XSS-equivalent dynamic JS)
patterns.semantic: inline event handler in shipped SVG/HTML — runs script when SVG renders
patterns.v2_replacement: extension contract should disallow shipping SVG with inline event handlers; use addEventListener at mount
patterns.decision_ref: yara svg_onload_onerror rule; high-volume hits across vendored & 1st-party JS bundles

patterns.surface: Obfuscated string-replace pipeline (often PowerShell, but pattern bleeds into JS minifiers)
patterns.semantic: indicator-of-compromise pattern — also a false-positive on heavily-minified bundles
patterns.v2_replacement: n/a — flag for human review; not a v2 contract change

patterns.surface: Python web/ template uses exec/eval to emit JS
patterns.semantic: server-side dynamic code that produces frontend payloads
patterns.v2_replacement: static templating only; no exec/eval at request time
patterns.decision_ref: bandit B102 fired under a frontend path
